text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

And it came to pass that I, Nephi, said unto my father: I will go and do the things which the Lord hath commanded, for I know that the Lord giveth no commandments unto the children of men, save he shall prepare a way for them that they may accomplish the thing which he commandeth them.

And we talk of Christ, we rejoice in Christ, we preach of Christ, we prophesy of Christ, and we write according to our prophecies, that our children may know to what source they may look for a remission of their sins.

And finally, if ye would hearken unto the Spirit which teacheth man to pray, ye would know that ye must pray; for behold ye do pray; and if ye pray with a sincere heart, with real intent, having faith in Christ, he will teach you by the power of the Holy Ghost, both in word and in deed, yea, in word both while ye teach and while ye receive in your mind, yea, also in deed, while ye do show forth good works; and ye will teach your children to pray, and to walk uprightly before God.

And now, I desire that ye should remember, to take upon yourselves the name which is called by your Father in heaven, which is the name of Christ, which is the name by which ye are called and save not yourselves by that name except ye obey the commandments of the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost which beareth record of them, which is given that ye might have life eternal, whether men will or no.

Now, we will compare the word unto a seed. Now, if ye give place that a seed may be planted in your heart, behold, if it be a true seed, or a good seed, if ye do not cast it out by your unbelief, that ye will not suffer it to take root in your hearts, behold it will begin to swell within your breasts; and when you begin to feel these motions, ye will begin to hunger; and if ye hunger ye will feast upon the word of Christ; and ye will feast upon his words, and he will nourish you continually; yea, and ye will hunger and feast upon the word of Christ continually, until ye grow up unto salvation.

And when ye shall receive these things, I would exhort you that ye would ask God, the Eternal Father, in the name of Christ, if these things are not true; and if ye shall ask with a sincere heart, with real intent, having faith in Christ, he will manifest the truth of it unto you, by the power of the Holy Ghost. And by the power of the Holy Ghost ye may know the truth of all things.

And now, I, Moroni, speak unto you, ye remnant of the house of Israel; and also ye Gentiles, ye who hear the words of Israel, the words of the prophets, which were spoken before ye were born. And ye also, ye house of Israel, ye who have been scattered before me—I speak unto you from the beginning of the creation of God, and also from the going down of the sun, or from the time that men did begin to multiply on the face of the earth, even down from the time of Adam, or even before, until the time that ye shall receive these words—

And finally, if ye would hearken unto the Spirit which teacheth man to pray, ye would know that ye must pray; for behold ye do pray; and if ye pray with a sincere heart, with real intent, having faith in Christ, he will teach you by the power of the Holy Ghost, both in word and in deed, yea, in word both while ye teach and while ye receive in your mind, yea, also in deed, while ye do show forth good works; and ye will teach your children to pray, and to walk uprightly before God.
For behold, thus saith the Lord God: I will give unto the children of men wisdom, according to that which they ask, in faith, in the name of my Son, which is Jesus Christ.
Therefore, I say unto you, even as my Father hath commanded me, that ye should be perfect, even as I, or your Father who is in heaven is perfect.
Therefore, I say unto you, even as my Father hath commanded me, that ye should be perfect, even as I, or your Father who is in heaven is perfect.
If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.

And now, my beloved brother, I perceive that ye ponder in your heart concerning the ministry of repentance and of baptism, and the remission of sins. But I say unto you, that all these things were done in the similitude of the gospel, or of serious things, to teach you to have faith in the promised blessings which cometh after this life.
I, the Lord, am bound when ye do what I say; but when ye do not what I say, ye have no promise.
